Symend建立了行为分析，将其与客户互动产品集成在一起，以帮助他们确定在支付账单方面遇到麻烦的客户，然后建议采用其他支付方式以防止他们完全违约，该公司已经获得了4300万美元的融资。
 这笔资金是其B系列的扩展，B系列于去年5月关闭，使该轮融资总额达到9500万美元，自2016年成立以来为加拿大卡尔加里初创公司提供了超过1亿美元。
   Symend没有透露其估值，但是与Tiffany Kaminsky（初创公司CMO）共同创立公司的Symend首席执行官Hanif Joshaghani表示，这是在投资于解决方案以帮助支持客户寻找解决方案的企业中出现的“大规模上升”之后的很难付款。
 他在接受采访时说：“客户的不确定性非常高，服务提供商没有能力以有效地引起客户共鸣并能使他们放心的方式来支持大量的咨询。”
 该初创公司一直主要与电信，金融服务，公用事业和媒体领域的客户合作，并声称拥有北美三分之二的主要电信提供商以及一家跨国银行作为客户。
 去年，Symend表示，它有望在2020年底之前与1亿客户（即其客户的客户）合作，我们正在询问该公司是否已经超过这个数字。
 这家初创公司的目标是双重的：它可以确定客户何时难以支付账单，并为客户提供除了简单的延期付款之外的其他选择，许多人推迟了解决该问题的计划。 
同时，Symend的解决方案不仅仅在于避免延期：该公司的解决方案旨在为客户服务运营中流量巨大的公司提供帮助和替代方案。
 但是，延期仍然是关键点：去年，大多数服务提供商一直在提供延期作为其避免违约的第一线方法。但Joshaghani表示，他认为延期“可能会给客户带来虚假的财务安全感”。
  该公司在7月对500名用户进行的一项调查发现，到2020年4月至2020年7月之间，账单支付滞后的人增加了27％，而这些人通常平均会支付三张账单：至少一项支付了55％贷款; 37％的抵押/租金落后； 21％的信用额度落后； 52％的信用卡付款落后。
 缺少付款和任何个人财务困难通常不仅与没有金钱联系在一起：可能会有工作损失，疾病，家庭麻烦，以及背后的原因为何某人发现自己错过了账单截止日期。
 因此，Symend试图采取一种敏感的方法来确定何时出现问题并加以解决。
 Joshaghani指出：“鉴于Symend的主要目标是使客户免受负面影响，因此我们与客户一起部署了战略，以确保客户获得支持并有权采取行动，并避免在延期期间堆积账单。” “通过向客户提供富有同情心的沟通，自助服务工具和灵活的还款方式，Symend帮助领先企业在原本充满不确定性和压力的时间内为客户提供了积极的体验。通过使客户能够通过数字工具采取行动，Symend缓解了不堪重负的呼叫中心带来的压力，提高了客户满意度，降低了运营成本，并帮助客户在收款之前解决了到期的账单。”
 如前所述，Symend的员工大约是25％的行为科学博士学位，但并未详细介绍其运作方式或评估客户的方法。它使用来自企业本身的数据，并将其与第三方资源结合在一起（与许多基于AI的金融科技公司可能用来例如权衡某人以特定利率获得特定贷款的资格的数据来源不同） 。 
Kaminsky指出，在业务的最后一年中，该公司对其算法和分析进行了更多投资。
 她说：“使用我们的行为告知算法，Symend可以识别和区分客户行为的关键变化，从而根据个人的独特偏好来个性化和优化交互。” “ Symend的AI / ML模型通过将来自客户互动的见解与所采取措施的历史数据相结合，进一步迈出了一步。这些见解有助于Symend发现潜在的心理和行为特征，从而确定哪种参与策略将对行为产生积极影响。”
 然后，为了捕获客户的情绪并进一步迭代这些策略，她说，Symend使用NLP处理模型根据提供的对通信的响应以及在自助服务工具中对情绪进行自动分类。她说：“我们的指标的最终目标是建立持续正面的品牌体验，远远超出应有的账单，这就是我们的AI / ML模型使用情感分析和参与度评分的原因，”她说。 “这最终确保沟通能够与客户的独特和不断变化的需求产生共鸣，从而创造出始终如一的积极体验，从而留住客户。”
 展望未来，该公司将利用这笔资金继续招聘，尤其是在国际范围内，重点是在拉丁美洲和亚太地区开设业务。它还将投资扩大产品范围，以包括客户保留和获取工具，同时还将加大投资以扩大产品范围，以帮助人们避免账单违约。
 Inovia Capital合伙人丹尼斯·卡维尔曼（Dennis Kavelman）在声明中说：“我们坚信Symend的使命是通过帮助客户避免收款来为企业增加持久价值。”他们的方法具有差异性和有效性，因为它结合了行为科学和数据科学，为每个客户提供个性化的方法。通过这项新的投资，Symend获得了充分的资金来执行其全球扩展战略，并与许多行业的大型企业合作。”
note_en:
Symend, which builds behavioral analytics that integrate with customer engagement products to help identify customers having trouble paying bills, and then suggests payment alternatives to keep them from defaulting altogether, has picked up $43 million in funding.
 The money is an extension to its Series B, closed in  May of last year, bringing the total for the round to $95 million, and over $100 million for the Calgary, Canada startup since being founded in 2016.
   Symend is not disclosing its valuation but Hanif Joshaghani, Symend’s CEO who co-founded the company with Tiffany Kaminsky (the startups CMO), said that it comes on the heels of a “massive uptick” among enterprises investing in solutions to help support customers finding it hard to meet payments.
 “Customer uncertainty has been extremely high and service providers don’t have the capacity to support the mass volumes of inquiries in a way that effectively resonates with and empathetically reassures customers,” he said in an interview.
 The startup has been working primarily with clients in telecommunications, financial services, utilities and media and claims to have two-thirds of the major telecommunications providers in North America, as well as a multinational bank, as customers.
 Last year, Symend said it was on track to work with 100 million customers (that is, customers of its clients) by the end of 2020, and we are asking the company whether it has passed that number.
 The startup’s aim is twofold: it identifies when their customers are having trouble paying bills, and offers those customers alternatives beyond simple deferrals, which for many just put off addressing the issue.
 At the same time, Symend’s solution is not just about avoiding deferrals: the company’s solutions aim to provide assistance and alternatives to companies that are seeing an overwhelming amount of traffic in its customer service operations.
 Deferrals, however, remain a key point: Most service providers have been offering deferral periods this past year as their first-line approach to avoiding defaults. But Joshaghani said he believes that deferrals “can provide a false sense of financial security for customers.”
  A survey the company conducted in July among 500 of its users found a 27% increase in people falling behind in bill payments between April and July 2020, with those falling behind typically doing so on an average of three bills: 55% on at least one loan; 37% were behind on mortgage/rent; 21% were behind on their line of credit; 52% were behind on credit card payments.
 Missing payments, and any personal financial trouble, is usually tied in with more than simply not having money: there can be job losses, illness, family trouble, and much more behind why someone finds himself or herself missing a bill deadline.
 Symend therefore tries to take a sensitive approach both to identifying when problems are arising, and addressing them.
 “Given that Symend’s primary goal is to save customers from negative outcomes, we deployed strategies with our clients to ensure customers felt supported and empowered to act and avoid bills piling up through the deferral period,” Joshaghani noted. “By engaging customers with empathetic communications, self-serve tools and flexible repayment options, Symend has helped leading enterprises provide customers with a positive experience in an otherwise highly uncertain and stressful time. By empowering customers to act through digital tools, Symend alleviates pressure from overwhelmed call centres, increases customer satisfaction, lowers operating costs and helps customers resolve past due bills before they reach collections.”
 As we have described before, Symend’s staff is around 25% behavioral science PhDs, but it does not go into a lot of detail about how it works, or the approach that it takes in evaluating customers. It uses data from businesses themselves, and combines that with third-party resources (not unlike the data trove that many AI-based fintechs might use to, say, weigh up the eligibility of a person for a particular loan at a particular interest rate).
 Kaminsky notes that the last year of business has seen the company make more investments into its algorithms and analytics.
 “Using our behaviorally informed algorithms, Symend can identify and distinguish between key variations in customer behavior to personalize and optimize interactions based on the unique preferences of the individual,” she said. “Symend’s AI/ML model takes this a step further by combining insights from customer interactions with historical data on actions taken. These insights help Symend discover underlying psychological and behavioral traits to determine which engagement strategies will positively shape behavior.”
 Then to capture customer sentiment and further iterate on these strategies, she says that Symend uses NLP processing models to automatically categorize sentiment based on provided responses to communications and within self-serve tools. “Our metrics are shaped with the end goal of building a consistently positive brand experience that extends far beyond past due bills, which is why our AI/ML model uses sentiment analysis and engagement scoring,” she said. “This ultimately ensures that communications resonate with the customer’s unique and evolving needs, creating a consistently positive experience that retains customers.”
 Going ahead, the company is going to use the funding to continue hiring, in particular internationally, with a focus on opening up operations in Latin Americas and Asia-Pacific regions. It’s also going to be investing in expanding its products to include customer retention and acquisition tools, alongside more investment in expanding its product to help people avoid defaults on bills.
 “We are big believers in Symend’s mission to add lasting value to enterprises, by helping their customers avoid collections,” said Dennis Kavelman, Partner at Inovia Capital, in a statement. “Their approach is differentiated and effective because it combines behavioral science and data science to drive a personalized approach for each customer. With this new investment, Symend is well funded to execute its strategy of scaling globally, and to partner with large enterprises in many industries.”
title: Symend获得4300万美元用于建立一个平台，以帮助客户避免账单违约
title_en: Symend nabs $43M for a platform to help customers avoid defaulting on bills

note:
在Fabric宣布完成950万美元的种子轮融资后不到四个月就宣布了这一消息。首席执行官Faisal Masud表示，Fabric并没有打算这么快地筹集更多资金，但鉴于其增长和投资者的兴趣，他的创业公司领导团队不得不问：“我们是推迟增长还是现在就走？”
 答案可能不言而喻，因为您正在阅读此资助案例。本轮融资由Norwest Venture Partners牵头，Norwest的Scott Beechuk加入了Fabric的董事会。 Redpoint Ventures和Sierra Ventures也参加了。
  Beechuk通过电子邮件告诉我：“ Fabric是唯一的无头贸易平台，无需拖放即可将其简单配置与强大的功能结合在一起，从而创建丰富的现代客户体验。” “但是，最重要的是，费萨尔（CEO）和[联合创始人Ryan Bartley]是来自亚马逊，eBay，Staples和Groupon的电子商务界的摇滚明星。他们对客户的挑战充满同情心，并拥有以出色的产品打乱成熟的电子商务市场的专业知识。”
 在大流行期间，电子商务发展迅速，并且有许多平台提供“无头”方法，正如Masud在博客文章中所述，该方法将购物体验的前端和后端分隔开。
 但是Masud表示，在支持成长中的大型品牌（无论是直接面向消费者还是企业对企业）方面，“当今市场仍有很大差距”。
  他说：“我们想说Shopify Plus是我们的入门，而Salesforce Commerce Cloud是我们的入门。”换句话说，Fabric为“ Shopify Plus规模已过时，但又不想在Salesforce Commerce Cloud上发大财”的企业服务。
 为了为这些客户提供服务，Fabric构建了32个独立的应用程序，这些应用程序可以与现有的商务工具集成在一起，并涵盖从客户体验到产品信息和订单管理的所有内容。 
Masud认为，其他大多数将自己标榜为无头电子商务平台的产品都是“杰瑞操纵自己变得无头”。
 他说：“它们建于2007年，不是灵活或模块化的。”尽管旧平台可能需要18个月才能吸引大客户，但Fabric只需数周。
 这家初创公司也不会阻止其客户进入亚马逊和其他市场。但是Masud（曾担任AmazonBasics和AmazonWarehouse的主管）表示，他们需要同时建立自己的差异化购物体验以及与消费者的直接关系。
 他说：“我建立了AmazonBasics，AmazonBasics即将为您服务。” “我们鼓励他们与众不同并继续其作为市场的旅程，与他们的客户建立直接业务。”
 有了新的资金，Fabric将能够扩大其销售和营销团队，同时还能继续改进产品。 Masud表示，他对吸引更大或更小的客户的兴趣不大，而对通过“构建更大的套件副驾驶应用程序”（例如通过做更多事情来支持订单物流和履行）的扩展更感兴趣。
note_en:
The announcement comes less than four months after  Fabric announced its $9.5 million seed round. CEO Faisal Masud said Fabric hadn’t intended to raise more funding so quickly, but given its growth and investor interest, he  the startup’s leadership team had to ask, “Do we delay the growth or do we just go now?”
 The answer is probably self-evident, since you’re reading this funding story. The round was led by Norwest Venture Partners, with Norwest’s Scott Beechuk joining Fabric’s board of directors. Redpoint Ventures and Sierra Ventures also participated.
  “Fabric is the only headless commerce platform that combines the simplicity of drag-and-drop configuration with the power to create rich modern customer experiences, without the need for a large dev team,” Beechuk told me via email. “Most importantly, however, is the fact that Faisal (CEO) and [co-founder Ryan Bartley] are rockstar veterans of the e-commerce world from Amazon, eBay, Staples and Groupon. They empathize with their customers’ challenges and have the expertise to disrupt the mature e-commerce market with a significantly better product.”
 E-commerce has  grown rapidly during the pandemic, and there are a number of platforms offering a “headless” approach, which (as  Masud explained in a blog post) separates the frontend and the backend of the shopping experience.
 But Masud said there’s still “a large gap in the market today” when it comes to supporting large, growing brands, whether they’re direct-to-consumer or business-to-business.
  “We like to say that Shopify Plus is our on-ramp and Salesforce Commerce Cloud is our off-ramp,” he said. In other words, Fabric serves businesses that have “outgrown Shopify Plus but don’t want to spend a fortune on Salesforce Commerce Cloud.”
 To serve those customers, Fabric has built 32 separate applications which can be integrated with existing commerce tools, and which collectively cover everything from customer experience to product information and order management.
 Masud argued that most other products billing themselves as headless e-commerce platforms are “jerry rigging themselves to become headless”.
 “They were built in 2007, they’re not flexible or modular,” he said, adding that while older platforms might require 18 months to on-board a large customer, Fabric only requires weeks.
 The startup isn’t discouraging its customers from having a presence on Amazon and other marketplaces, either. But Masud (who was previously director of AmazonBasics and AmazonWarehouse) said they need to build their own differentiated shopping experiences and their own direct relationship with consumers at the same time.
 “I built AmazonBasics and AmazonBasics is coming for you,” he said. “We encourage them to differentiate and continue their journey as a marketplace, building a direct business with their customer.”
 With the new funding, Fabric will be able to expand its sales and marketing team while also continuing to improve the product. Masud said he’s less interested in reaching larger or smaller customers, and more interested in expansion by “building out a bigger suite co-pilot apps,” for example by doing more to support order logistics and fulfillment.
title: “无头”电子商务平台Fabric融资4300万美元

note:
SentinelOne整理大量数据以帮助客户了解他们的安全状况，并拥有一个使工程师能够快速迭代数据并找到问题根源的工具，对于他们，CEO和联合创始人而言将是极为宝贵的Tomer Weingarten解释道。他告诉我：“我们认为Scalyr非常适合我们对如何为每个企业（客户）大规模保护数据的持续愿景。”
 他说，他们花了大量时间在一家可以满足其独特扩展需求的公司上，当他们遇到Scalyr时，他们很快就发现了建立实时数据湖的公司的潜力。 “当我们看技术的规模时，我们显然在全世界搜寻了最好的数据分析技术。我们[相信]当我们找到一个可以提取数据并使其实时访问的平台时，我们发现了一个令人难以置信的特殊之处。” Weingarten解释说。
   他认为实时元素可以改变游戏规则，因为它使客户能够防止违规行为，而不仅仅是对违规行为做出反应。 “如果您正在考虑减轻攻击或对攻击做出反应，那么您可以实时进行操作，并且可以实时处理数据，并实时找到异常情况然后加以处理，那么您就变成了一个系统实际上可以转移攻击，而不仅仅是看到攻击并对其做出反应。”他解释说。
 该公司将Scalyr视为可以集成到该平台的产品，但仍将保持独立。这意味着现有客户应该能够像以前一样继续使用Scalyr，同时受益于有更大的公司为其研发做贡献。
 根据Crunchbase的数据，虽然SentinelOne不是一家上市公司，但它是一家相当可观的私人公司，已经筹集了超过6.95亿美元。该公司最近的一轮融资是在去年2月，投资额为2亿美元，估值为11亿美元。
 至于Scalyr，它是由史蒂夫·纽曼（Steve Newman）于2011年推出的，史蒂夫·纽曼（Steve Newman）首先构建了一个名为Writely的文字处理器，并于2006年将其出售给了Google。这实际上就是后来成为Google Docs的基础。纽曼坚持不懈，并开始构建可扩展Google Docs的基础架构，他利用这些经验和知识来构建Scalyr。根据Crunchbase的数据，这家初创公司筹集了2700万美元，其中包括2017年的2000万美元A轮投资。
 该交易将在本季度完成，届时，Scalyr的45名员工将加入SentinalOne。
note_en:
SentinelOne sorts through oodles of data to help customers understand their security posture, and having a tool that enables engineers to iterate rapidly in the data, and get to the root of the problem is going to be extremely valuable for them, CEO and co-founder Tomer Weingarten explained. “We thought Scalyr would be just an amazing fit to our continued vision in how we secure data at scale for every enterprise [customer] out there,” he told me.
 He said they spent a lot of time shopping for a company that could meet their unique scaling needs and when they came across Scalyr, they saw the potential pretty quickly with a company that has built a real-time data lake. “When we look at the scale of our technology, we obviously scoured the world to find the best data analytics technology out there. We [believe] we found something incredibly special when we found a platform that can ingest data, and make it accessible in real time,” Weingarten explained.
   He believes the real time element is a game changer because it enables customers to prevent breaches, rather than just reacting to them. “If you’re thinking about mitigating attacks or reacting to attacks, if you can do that in real time and you can process data in real time, and find the anomalies in real time and then meet them, you’re turning into a system that can actually deflect the attacks and not just see them and react to them,” he explained.
 The company sees Scalyr as a product they can integrate into the platform, but also one which will remain a stand-alone. That means existing customers should be able to continue using Scalyr as before, while benefiting from having a larger company contributing to its R&D.
 While SentinelOne is not a public company, it is a pretty substantial private one, having raised over $695 million, according to Crunchbase data. The company’s most recent funding round came in February last year,  a $200 million investment with a $1.1 billion valuation.
 As for Scalyr it was launched in 2011 by Steve Newman, who first built a word processor called Writely and  sold it to Google in 2006. It was actually the basis for what became Google Docs. Newman stuck around and started building the infrastructure to scale Google Docs, and he used that experience and knowledge to build Scalyr. The startup raised $27 million along the way, according to Crunchbase data including  a $20 million Series A investment in 2017.
 The deal will close this quarter, and when it does Scalyr’s 45 employees will be joining SentinalOne.
title: SentinelOne将以1.55亿美元收购高速测井初创公司Scalyr
title_en: SentinelOne to acquire high-speed logging startup Scalyr for $155M

note:
或者说该公司将最终从Breakthrough Energy Ventures筹集资金，该公司是亿万富翁支持的投资工具，专注于为开发减少温室气体排放的技术的融资公司以及一些全球最大的工业和石油天然气公司。
 当时，琼斯（Jones）在南达科他州南部的小型投资公司Beryllium Capital工作，并确定了C-Zero的潜在投资机会，这是一家由UCSB教授埃里克·麦克法兰开发的商业化制氢新方法的公司。
  只有一个问题-麦克法兰（McFarland）进行了研究，但不知道如何经营公司。那时琼斯介入了。他的公司没有进行投资，但是当前经济学家科学作家接手时，该公司得以从PG＆amp; E和加利福尼亚的两家大型公用事业公司SoCal Gas获得种子轮。
 他们进行投资的原因与Breakthrough Energy Ventures对这家年轻公司产生兴趣的原因相同。即使可再生能源生产以惊人的速度上线，但在可预见的未来，世界上许多地区仍将使用化石燃料，而化石燃料生产所产生的温室气体排放量必须降至零。
 C-Zero正在开发一项技术，将天然气转化为氢，这是一种更为清洁的燃料来源，而固态碳是唯一的废物流，可用于发电，工艺加热以及生产氢和氨等大宗化学产品。
   该公司的技术是甲烷热解的一种形式，它使用专有的化学催化剂将氢气与其他颗粒分离，从而留下了固体碳废物。该过程既不浪费，也不存在固体碳，也不可再生（原料是天然气），比目前的低成本制氢方法更清洁，而且比更可再生的制氢方法便宜。
 产生可再生氢需要使电能通过水发送电荷，以将液体分解为氢和氧。从氢原子中吸取氢原子比从碳原子中分裂出氢要多得多。 
琼斯说：“氢之所以有趣，是因为它是间歇性可再生能源的重要补充。” “这实际上与能量存储有关……当您每天和季节性查看长期存储时……它变得非常昂贵。拥有化学燃料将是使一切脱碳的关键部分。”
 琼斯将这项技术描述为“燃烧前碳捕集”，并认为释放氢在众多工业应用（包括重型车辆加油，公用事业发电和制造用工业动力）中的优势可能至关重要。
  “每年生产的商品氢超过1,000亿美元，” C-Zero的1,150万美元资金的新主要投资方Breakthrough Energy Ventures的Carmichael Roberts说。 “不幸的是，绝大部分生产来自称为蒸汽甲烷重整的过程，该过程还会产生大量的二氧化碳。寻找低成本，低排放的制氢方法（例如一种C-Zero发明的方法），对于释放该分子使农业，化学，制造和运输领域的主要部分脱碳的潜力至关重要。”
 新一轮加入由比尔·盖茨支持的Breakthrough Energy Ventures的是Eni Next，这是意大利油气和电力公司Mitsubishi Heavy Industries以及专注于氢技术的风险投资公司AP Ventures的投资部门。
 三菱重工已经为C-Zero的技术提供了应用。该公司正在对现有的燃煤电厂进行供电，以期在2025年之前使用天然气和氢气的混合物。C-Zero的技术有可能帮助实现这一目标。
 除了用于制氢的低成本方法外，C-Zero可能是最早获得美国国税局（IRS）今年初在美国建立的碳封存新税收抵免资格的公司之一。这些信用将为符合资格的公司每吨固存的固体碳20美元，这是C-Zero工艺产生的确切废物。
 即使C-Zero开始将其技术商业化，它也面临着来自世界上一些最大的化学公司的激烈竞争。 
德国化工巨头巴斯夫近十年来一直在开发自己的甲烷热解风味产品，并已开始建设测试设施以扩大其自身清洁氢气的生产。
 昨天，另外两家欧洲大公司也参加了制氢比赛，法国化学制品公司液化空气集团宣布与西门子能源成立一家合资企业，致力于制氢。
 琼斯承认，该公司的技术目前只是权宜之计。未来，随着世界从废物转向可再生天然气生产，他设想了潜在的循环氢经济的潜力。
 “这项技术会在100年后问世吗？如果这样的话，那是因为我们使用的是可再生天然气。”琼斯说。到达那里需要走很多步骤，但琼斯对项目的近期成功充满信心。
 “总会需要非常高能量密度的燃料。液态氢是自然界中核能以外最密集的能量。”他说。 “我认为氢会保留下来。归根结底，要避免的二氧化碳成本最低的能源成本最低，这将是赢家。”
note_en:
Or that the company would wind up raising money from Breakthrough Energy Ventures, the billionaire backed investment vehicle focused on financing companies developing technologies to reduce greenhouse gas emissions, and some of the world’s largest industrial and oil and gas companies.
 At the time, Jones was working for Beryllium Capital, a small investment office out of South Dakota, and had identified a potential investment opportunity in C-Zero, a company commercializing a new way of making hydrogen developed by  Eric McFarland, a professor at UCSB.
  There was only one problem — McFarland had the research, but didn’t know how to run a company. That’s when Jones stepped in. His firm didn’t make the investment, but when the former Economist science writer took over, the company was able to nab a seed round from PG&E and SoCal Gas, California’s two massive utilities.
 The reason for their investments is the same reason Breakthrough Energy Ventures became interested in the young company. Even with renewable energy production coming on line at a breakneck pace, much of the world will still be using fossil fuels for the foreseeable future and the greenhouse gas emissions from that fossil fuel production needs to go to zero.
 C-Zero is developing a technology that converts natural gas to hydrogen, a much cleaner source of fuel, and solid carbon as the only waste stream for use in  electrical generation, process heating and the production of commodity chemicals like hydrogen and ammonia.
   The company’s technology is a form of methane pyrolysis, which uses a proprietary chemical catalyst to separate the hydrogen gas from other particles, leaving behind that solid carbon waste. The process, which is neither waste free (there’s that solid carbon) nor renewable (the feedstock is natural gas), is cleaner than current low-cost methods of hydrogen production and far cheaper than the more renewable ways of making hydrogen.
 Making renewable hydrogen requires making electricity to send a charge through water to split the liquid into hydrogen and oxygen. And it takes far more energy to pull a hydrogen atom off of an oxygen atom than it does to split that hydrogen from a carbon atom.
 “The reason that hydrogen is interesting is that it is a great supplement to intermittent renewables,” said Jones. “It’s really about energy storage… when you look at long duration storage on a daily and seasonal basis… it becomes exorbitantly expensive. Having a chemical fuel is going to be critical part of decarbonizing everything.”
 Jones describes the technology as “pre-combustion carbon capture”, and thinks that it could be critical to unlocking the benefits of hydrogen for a range of industrial applications including heavy vehicle fueling, utility power generation, and industrial power for manufacturing.
  “Over $100 billion of commodity hydrogen is produced annually,” said Carmichael Roberts, Breakthrough Energy Ventures, the new lead investor in C-Zero’s $11.5 million funding. “Unfortunately, the overwhelming majority of that production comes from a process called steam methane reforming, which also produces large quantities of CO2. Finding low cost, low emission methods of hydrogen production – such as the one C-Zero has created – will be critical to unlocking the molecule’s potential to decarbonize major segments of the agricultural, chemical, manufacturing and transportation sectors.”
 Joining the Bill Gates-backed Breakthrough Energy Ventures in the new round is Eni Next, the investment arm of the Italian oil and gas and power company, Mitsubishi Heavy Industries, and the hydrogen technology-focused venture firm, AP Ventures.
 Mitsubishi Heavy Industries already has an application for C-Zero’s technology. The company is in the process of re-powering an existing coal plant to run on a combination of natural gas and hydrogen by 2025. It’s possible that C-Zero’s technology could help get there.
 Beyond the lower cost methods used in manufacturing hydrogen, C-Zero may be one of the first companies that could qualify for new tax credits on carbon sequestration established by the IRS in the U.S. earlier this year. Those credits would give qualifying companies $20 per ton of sequestered solid carbon — the exact waste product from C-Zero’s process.
 Even as C-Zero begins commercializing its technology it faces some stiff competition from some of the largest chemical companies in the world.
 The German chemicals giant BASF has been developing its own flavor of methane pyrolysis for nearly a decade and has begun building test facilities to scale up production of its own clean hydrogen.
 And yesterday, two other big European corporations are also joining the hydrogen production game as the French chemicals company Air Liquide announced a joint venture with Siemens Energy to work on hydrogen production.
 Jones acknowledges that the company’s technology is only a stopgap solution… for now. In the future, as the world moves to renewable natural gas production from waste, he envisions the potential of a potentially circular hydrogen economy.
 “ In 100 years will this technology be around? If it is it’ll be because we’re using renewable natural gas,” Jones said. There are a lot of steps that need to be traveled to get there, but Jones is confident in the near-term success of the project.
 “There’s always going to be  a need for a very energy dense fuel. Liquid hydrogen is the most energy dense thing that’s out there outside of something that’s nuclear in nature,” he said. “I think that hydrogen is here to stay. At the end of the day the lowest cost of energy that has the lowest cost for avoided CO2 is what’s going to win.”
title: 一家使用新技术制造氢气的初创公司从比尔·盖茨的气候技术基金中提取现金
title_en: A startup using a new tech to make hydrogen extracts cash from Bill Gates’ climate tech fund

note:
在对苹果令人印象深刻的M1芯片沉默了数月之后，英特尔刚刚以精心设计的Arm芯片为基础进行了鼓掌。不好看
  第11代Core i7-1185G7在本机和非本机应用程序中都可以与MacBook Pro中的M1媲美或大大超过M1的性能。
  MacBook Pro不会通过认证成为Evo笔记本电脑。
  新款MacBook具有多种兼容性问题，从多台显示器到游戏控制器，以及许多已记录的软件插件问题。
 用我们这个时代的话来说，它又是“开枪”，英特尔特别强调了竞争产品中的问题。去年11月，英特尔对AMD的Ryzen 4000进行了同样的处理，发现在许多情况下使用电池供电时，性能都会下降。
 外界观察家和顽固的苹果迷的正常反应是，将这些挑战视为酸葡萄。但是，英特尔表示有基准收入。由于我们喜欢与真人秀打架，因此我们将详细介绍英特尔的主张，并告诉您是否相信它们。
   英特尔表示，在WebXPRT 3测试中，英特尔在Core i7系统和Arm-native MacBook上使用了相同版本的Chrome。英特尔芯片在WebXPRT 3中处于领先地位，而x86芯片在完成照片增强测试时的速度快了将近三倍。 
不过，英特尔不仅使用WebXPRT 3。它还显示了Core i7在PowerPoint到PDF的导出中以及在多个Excel宏中以2.3倍的倍数对M1进行敲击。是的，英特尔使用Arm的本地版本的Office进行测试。
 我们的看法：我们确实需要进行类似的测试，但是我们不会怀疑结果。是的，您可能会认为一家市值数十亿美元的公司与另一家市值数十亿美元的公司公开竞争意味着“这都是假基准”，但事实并非如此，因为英特尔可能面临巨大的责任风险。这就是Diet Diet可口可乐不会直接将Diet Pepsi（百事可乐）称为不可饮用的even水的原因（即使确实如此）。
 您可以质疑的是，英特尔是否选择了支持其自身CPU设计的任务。但是即使您相信了，它也不会使测试无效，因为导出到PDF大约是您可以得到的真实的（尽管很无聊）。
  如果我们没有指出WebXPRT 3给英特尔带来了在本国的优势，我们也将不为所动，因为基准测试开发人员（Principled Technologies）与英特尔长期以来一直保持着联系。但是，那不一定意味着基准测试会偏爱英特尔，因为AMD的Zen 3目前领先于英特尔的移动CPU。尽管如此，苹果粉丝肯定会说这里的裁判错过了一些犯规行为。
   对于内容创建任务，英特尔展示出在执行4K AVC到HEVC / H.265文件转换时，Core i7比M1快约1.12倍。在此基准测试中，他们拥有使用M1本机版本的Handbrake的MacBook。
 但是真正的破坏是一旦您进入Topaz Lab的Gigapixel AI和Denoise AI发生的，英特尔酷睿芯片将M1压碎在基于AI的噪声消除和扩大中。或者“压碎”这个词太好用了，因为它更像是Core i7远远超过了M1，M1希望它从未设计过。
 我们的看法：就视频转换而言，我们认为这两个平台之间的距离太近了，如果我们是英特尔的话，我们可能不会高兴。但是，在AI功能上，我们认为结果是非常真实的，因为我们已经通过自己的M1 vs Core i7和Ryzen测试亲眼目睹了它。第11代酷睿i7-1185G7彻底击败了AMD的Ryzen 4000U和苹果的M1，我们考虑过打电话给911，让某人出门进行福利检查。 
我们应该指出，Topaz Lab的应用程序属于少数几个（但仍然令人印象深刻）的应用程序，这些应用程序旨在利用Intel CPU内部的硬件加速。对硬件进行优化后，您将获得无数的性能提升。这就是使Apple手机长久发光的魔力。
  英特尔还使用自动重组中的beta M1本机版本在Adobe Premiere Pro中处于领先地位，并导出到H.264和H.265。它们是不错的胜利，但是，Mac上的代码仍处于测试阶段。也就是说，英特尔指出，Beta版完全禁用了诸如Content Aware Fill之类的重要功能，这是一个问题。如果Photoshop的本机版本问世，并且缺少一些关键功能，那么对于Apple（和Adobe）来说就是一个巨大的问题。
   对于游戏而言，在实际可在MacBook上运行的游戏中，我们看到Apple M1和Core i7之间有些来回摆动。不过，英特尔并没有让它止步于此，而是决定通过向众多游戏展示MacBook使其得分为0来使苹果进一步尴尬，因为游戏支持不存在。英特尔指出，“没有更多的游戏”“无法在M1上运行”，然后在很大程度上衡量，它以另外10个无法在M1 MacBook上玩的游戏冲入苹果的行列：
  我们的观点：好的，我们明白了。 Mac吸引了游戏。我们只能说没有理性的人会不同意。在游戏方面，与PC相比，M1 Mac作为游戏平台实际上要低10英尺-除非您可能算上运行GeForce Now云游戏？
  您知道这个奇特的Intel Evo程序试图在惹恼消费者的关键领域提高笔记本电脑的性能吗？好吧，英特尔几乎说过，如果苹果将M1 MacBook提交给华硕，戴尔，惠普，联想，微星，宏cer和其他公​​司通过的同一程序，那将被拒绝。
 原因？英特尔表示，M1 MacBook的处理速度太慢会激怒消费者，例如Outlook中的``切换到日历''，PowerPoint中的``开始视频会议缩放''和``选择图片菜单''。
 我们的看法：我们也不知道具有Evo认证的基于AMD的笔记本电脑。因此，尽管英特尔可能认为拒绝Evo认证是对苹果的侮辱，但我们怀疑苹果不会在乎其笔记本电脑的徽章。 
也许最令人震惊的说法是英特尔展示了电池寿命问题。虽然性能测试可以由希望证明结果的人员选择，但电池寿命通常不会引起争议。
 苹果公司的官方说法是，使用苹果电视应用程序观看亮度为“从底部8次点击”的1080p视频时，M1 MacBook的电池续航时间长达18小时。苹果还声称，使用相同的“ 8次点击”条件，最多可以浏览15个小时的25个“热门”网站。
 但是，当英特尔将MacBook Air M1与配备酷睿i7-1165G7的Acer Swift 5进行对比时，发现两者基本都死了。 MacBook Air耗时10小时12分钟，而Acer Swift 5耗时10小时6分钟。
 区别？英特尔表示，它使用Safari观看了Netflix流，同时打开了选项卡，屏幕设置为相对较亮的250尼特。在Acer上，Safari被Chrome取代了，但亮度和Netflix保持不变。
 英特尔确实补充说，苹果公司的MacBook Air的“ 8次点击”大约为125尼特，非常暗。
 我们的看法：我们同意125尼特是用于视频测试的相当愚蠢的亮度。毕竟，谁想在笔记本电脑上“观看”电影，然后将屏幕调暗到您几乎看不到的大部分？
 此处最大的中断可能是Apple TV应用之间的差异，Apple TV应用可能是经过高度优化的而不是Safari，但是很难相信Safari尚未进行大幅优化。我们真的很想在这里进行其他测试。 
英特尔不仅仅关注M1的性能。它还说，它发现MacBook Pro存在严重缺陷，例如无法在Thunderbolt扩展坞上使用多个显示器。
 英特尔表示，虽然PC可以使用游戏耳机，eGPU，第三方指纹读取器，Wacom Drawing平板电脑和Xbox Controller，但它发现MacBook Pro根本不能与eGPU一起使用，并且在其他设备上存在多个问题。
 那只是硬件不兼容。英特尔与苹果的说唱战也凸显了Ableton，Bitwig Studio，Avid Pro Tools，FL Studio，Motu等许多插件的问题。
 我们的看法：MacBook只能使用一个外接显示器这一事实是一个大问题。我们想验证一下自己，但这在这个多显示器的世界中是一个严重的弱点。希望这只是新平台中的错误，但就我们所知，这也是硬件的局限性。
 长期以来，我们一直认为缺少eGPU是一个主要弱点，因为谁不想为游戏带来更高的性能？但公平地说：我们已经强调Mac对于游戏而言毫无价值，那么您到底需要eGPU做什么呢？
 我们认为，英特尔在软件问题上的看法略有减少。当您举足轻重，并告诉所有开发人员再次为另一种新体系结构重写软件时……事情将会破裂。
 对于M1 MacBook用户而言，好消息是这些应用程序和插件最终可能会得到修复。请注意，这并不意味着所有内容都会免费修复。我们已经看到开发人员利用这个机会（可能是有道理的）修复了下一版本中的问题，顺便说一句，您必须再次购买。 
英特尔没有讲到一点，我们知道为什么是声学。 新款MacBook M1的奇妙功能之一是几乎没有风扇噪音（它在那里，您可以使用Topaz Lab的产品来消除噪音），但是每条评论都正确地表扬了苹果公司无声的静音操作。 当前的PC失去了这种优势。  尽管有些人会认为英特尔对苹果的挖矿行为没有体面，但我们不同意。 苹果公司是第一个毫不客气地将英特尔抛弃在舷外的人，首先扔掉泥浆，因此，苹果有权将泥浆扔回苹果。 尽管如此，我们还是想测试一下一些英特尔更严格的硬件，以及电池寿命方面的兼容性。  注意：如果您在点击我们文章中的链接后购买商品，我们可能会赚取少量佣金。 阅读我们的会员链接政策以获取更多详细信息。
note_en:
After months of silence about Apple’s impressive M1 chip, Intel just clapped back with a carefully crafted takedown of the Arm-based chip. It isn’t pretty.
  An 11th gen Core i7-1185G7 can match or greatly exceed the M1’s performance in a MacBook Pro in both native and non-native applications.
  The MacBook Pro wouldn’t pass muster to be certified as an Evo laptop.
  The new MacBooks have a range of compatibility issues, from multiple monitor to game controllers , as well as many documented software plug-in problems.
 In the parlance of our time, it’s “shots fired” yet again, with Intel highlighting problems in competing products. In November, Intel did the same with AMD’s Ryzen 4000, which it found to suffer degraded performance when  running on battery in many situations.
 The normal reaction from outside observers and die-hard Apple fanboys is to dismiss these challenges as sour grapes. Intel, however, says it has benchmark receipts. Since we enjoy a good reality TV fight, we’ll detail Intel’s claims and tell you whether to believe them or not.
   Intel says in the WebXPRT 3 test, using the same version of Chrome for both the Core i7 system as well as the Arm-native MacBook, Intel takes the lead. The Intel chip was largely ahead in WebXPRT 3, and the x86 chip was nearly three times faster in finishing the photo enhancement test.
 Intel doesn’t just use WebXPRT 3, though. It also shows the Core i7 pummeling the M1 in a PowerPoint-to-PDF export, and in multiple Excel macros by a factor of 2.3x. And yes, Intel used the Arm-native versions of Office for its tests.
 Our take: We’d really need to run similar tests, but we don’t doubt the results. Yes, you may think one multi-billion dollar company publicly beefing with another multi-billion dollar company means “it’s all fake benchmarks,” but that’s probably not the case because of the huge liability risk Intel might face. That’s why Diet Coke doesn’t outright call Diet Pepsi undrinkable swill (even though it is.)
 What you  can question is whether Intel picked tasks that favor its own CPU design. But even if you believed that, it doesn’t invalidate the testing, because exporting to a PDF is about as real—albeit boring—as you can get.
  We’d also be remiss if we didn’t point out that WebXPRT 3 gives home-field advantage to Intel, as the benchmark developer (Principled Technologies) and Intel have long had a relationship. That, however, doesn’t necessarily mean the benchmark favors Intel, as  AMD’s Zen 3 currently has the lead over Intel’s mobile CPUs. Nonetheless, Apple fans are certain to say some fouls got missed by the refs here.
   For content creation tasks, Intel showed the Core i7 to be about 1.12x faster than the M1 in performing a 4K AVC-to-HEVC/H.265 file conversion. In this benchmark, they had the MacBook using the M1-native version of Handbrake.
 But the real destruction happens once you get to Topaz Lab’s Gigapixel AI and Denoise AI, with the Intel Core chip crushing the M1 in AI-based noise removal and enlargement. Or maybe “crushing” is too nice a term, as it’s more like the Core i7 outpaces the M1 by so much, the M1 wishes it had never been designed.
 Our take: As far as the video conversion goes, we think it’s too close to call between both platforms, and probably not something we’d gloat about if we were Intel. However, on the AI features, we think the results are pretty real, because we have witnessed it ourselves with our own  M1 vs Core i7 and Ryzen tests. The 11th gen Core i7-1185G7 so thoroughly thrashes AMD’s Ryzen 4000U and Apple’s M1, we thought about calling 911 for someone to go out to the house and do a welfare check.
  We should point out that Topaz Lab’s apps are among the few (but nonetheless impressive) apps that were designed to take advantage of the hardware acceleration inside of Intel’s CPUs. When you have optimization for your hardware, you get tons of performance gains. This is the same magic that’s made Apples phones shine for so long.
  Intel also gives itself the lead in Adobe Premiere Pro, using the beta M1 native version in Auto Reframe, exporting to H.264 and H.265. They’re decent wins, but come on, the code is still in beta for the Mac. That said, Intel points out that important features like Content Aware Fill are outright disabled on the beta version, and that’s a concern. If the native version of Photoshop comes out, and there are critical features missing from it, that’s a huge problem for Apple (and Adobe).
   For gaming, we see a bit of a back and forth between the Apple M1 and Core i7 in games that actually work on the MacBook. Intel doesn’t let it end there, though, and decides to embarrass Apple further by showing the numerous games where the MacBook scores a 0 because game support just doesn’t exist. Intel points out that “countless more” games “don’t run on the M1,” and then for good measure, it rushes Apple’s bench with a list 10 more games you can’t play on the M1 MacBook:
  Our take: OK, we get it. Macs suck for gaming. All we can is say no rational person would disagree. For gaming, the M1 Mac is actually about 10 feet below inferior as a gaming platform compared to the PC—unless maybe you count running GeForce Now cloud gaming on it?
  You know that fancy Intel Evo program that tries to improve laptop performance in key areas that annoy consumers? Well, Intel pretty much says that if Apple submitted the M1 MacBook to the same program that Asus, Dell, HP, Lenovo, MSI, Acer and others go through, it would be rejected.
 The reason? Intel says the M1 MacBook is too slow in doing things that anger consumers, such as “switch to Calendar” in Outlook, “start video conference Zoom” and “select picture menu” in PowerPoint.
 Our take: We know of no AMD-based laptops with Evo certification either. So while Intel might think it’s an insult to Apple to deny Evo certification, we suspect Apple won’t care about one less badge on its laptops.
   Perhaps the most shocking claim Intel showed deals with battery life. While performance tests can be cherry picked by those looking to prove an outcome, battery life usually can’t be disputed.
 Apple’s official claim gives the M1 MacBook up to 18 hours of battery life using Apple TV app to watch a 1080p video with the brightness set to “8 clicks from the bottom.” Apple also claims up to 15 hours browsing 25 “popular” websites with the same “8 clicks” criteria.
 When Intel pitted a MacBook Air M1 against an Acer Swift 5 with a Core i7-1165G7, however, it found both basically dead even. The MacBook Air came in at 10 hours and 12 minutes, and the Acer Swift 5 lasted 10 hours and 6 minutes.
 The difference? Intel said it used Safari to watch a Netflix stream with tabs open with the screen set to a relatively bright 250 nits. On the Acer, Safari was subbed out for Chrome, but the brightness and Netflix remained the same.
 Intel did add that Apple’s “8 clicks up” is about 125 nits of brightness on the MacBook Air which is pretty dim.
 Our take: We agree 125 nits is a pretty silly brightness to use for video testing. After all, who wants to “watch” a movie on a laptop but then dims the screen so much you can’t actually  see most of it?
 Probably the largest disconnect here might be the difference between the Apple TV app, which could possibly be highly optimized instead of Safari, but it’s hard to believe Safari would not be heavily optimized already. We’d really want to do additional testing of our own here.
   Intel didn’t just get into the performance of the M1. It also said it found the MacBook Pro had serious shortcomings, such as an inability to use more than one display with a Thunderbolt dock.
 And while the PC can use gaming headsets, eGPUs, a third-party finger print reader, Wacom Drawing tablet and Xbox Controller, Intel said it found the MacBook Pro simply doesn’t work with eGPUs, and had multiple issues with other devices.
 That’s just hardware incompatibility. Intel’s rap battle with Apple also highlights issues with plug-ins for Ableton, Bitwig Studio, Avid Pro Tools, FL Studio, Motu and many others.
 Our take: The fact that the MacBook can only use a single external display is a big problem. We’d want to verify that ourselves, but it’s a serious weakness in this multi-monitor world. Hopefully, it’s just a bug from the new platform, but for all we know, it’s a limitation of the hardware too.
 We’ve long said lack of eGPU is a major weakness because who doesn’t want more performance for gaming? But to be fair: We’ve already stressed the Mac is pretty worthless for gaming, so what exactly do you need that eGPU for?
 Intel’s take on the software issues is a little less problematic, we think. When you pull up stakes and tell all of your developers to yet again rewrite software for yet another new architecture... well, things will break.
 The good news for M1 MacBook users is these applications and plugins will likely get fixed eventually. Mind you, this doesn’t mean it’ll all get fixed for free. We’ve seen developers use the opportunity (which might be justified) to fix issues in the next version which, by the way, you have to buy again.
  One point Intel didn’t cover, and we know why, is acoustics. One of the singular amazing features of the new MacBook M1’s is the near lack of fan noise (it’s there and you can kick it off by using Topaz Lab’s products), but every review has properly praised Apple’s dead silent operation. That’s something current PCs lose hands down.
  While some will tut-tut that Intel’s digs at Apple are undignified, we disagree. Apple is the one who first flung mud when it unceremoniously dumped Intel overboard, so it’s within Intel’s right to fling mud back. Nonetheless, we would want to test out some of Intel’s more serious hardware compatibly and battery life claims.
   Note: When you purchase something after clicking links in our articles, we may earn a small commission. Read our  affiliate link policy for more details.
title: 英特尔基准测试称苹果的M1速度不快
title_en: Intel Benchmarks Say Apple's M1 Isn't Faster

note: 是的，您可以不受限制地购买氢氧化钠，钠和其他类似危险的化学药品，但数量很少，但是当我们过去使用直立的碱液洗衣服时，它们又是常见的家庭危害，许多人被化学灼伤，孩子们 喝着它，以为白色溶液是牛奶等，并且喉咙结疤了。 我们不再将这些化学物质用作普通家庭用品是有原因的，在公共抽水站的喷嘴中分配腐蚀性极强的可燃化学物质显然不是一个好主意。 在您身上洒一点汽油会使您的衣服变脏，变臭–在您身上洒一点氢化镁浆会使您的衣服消失。
note_en: Yes, you can buy sodium hydroxide, sodium, and other similarly dangerous chemicals without restrictions in small amounts – but they were also common household hazards back when we used to use straight up lye to wash our clothes, with many people getting chemical burns, kids drinking it thinking the white solution was milk, etc. and having their throats scarred shut. There is a reason why we don’t use these chemicals as common household items anymore, and dispensing extremely caustic and combustible chemicals out of a nozzle at a public pumping station is obviously not a good idea. Spilling a bit of gasoline on you makes your clothes stained and smelly – spilling a bit of magnesium hydride paste on you makes your clothes go away.
